summary.title: Telehealth Web Application Threat Model (Finished)
summary.description: Completed Task B model with Level 0, Level 1 and Level 2 DFDs plus starter STRIDE threats.

threats.title: Patient account spoofing
threats.description: Attacker impersonates a patient using stolen credentials or tokens.
threats.mitigation: MFA, short session lifetime, device anomaly detection.

threats.title: Clinician token replay
threats.description: Replayed clinician session token could issue fraudulent prescriptions.
threats.mitigation: Sender-constrained tokens, replay detection, step-up auth.

threats.title: PHI disclosure
threats.description: Authorization or storage failures could expose sensitive medical data.
threats.mitigation: ABAC/RBAC, encryption at rest, strict API auth checks.

threats.title: Third-party API tampering
threats.description: Manipulated responses from external integrations can affect clinical workflows.
threats.mitigation: TLS pinning, schema validation, signed API responses where available.

threats.title: Third-party API tampering
threats.description: Manipulated responses from external integrations can affect clinical workflows.
threats.mitigation: TLS pinning, schema validation, signed API responses where available.

threats.title: Credential stuffing
threats.description: Automated attempts can bypass weak auth controls.
threats.mitigation: Rate limiting, MFA, lockout, anomaly detection.

threats.title: Credential stuffing
threats.description: Automated attempts can bypass weak auth controls.
threats.mitigation: Rate limiting, MFA, lockout, anomaly detection.
